llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: This is a natural experiment comparing countries where ChatGPT was available vs. banned. The treatment group consists of 120 countries with ChatGPT access, control group is 27 countries without access.
llm_extracted.notes: Primary outcome is 'Num Pushes per 100k' (git pushes per 100,000 people). Table 6.1 Panel A reports DID coefficient of 899.268*** (SE=147.395) with baseline mean of 741.5. This is a level-transformed outcome (not log), so the coefficient represents the absolute increase in pushes per 100k. The baseline mean is explicitly stated as 'Baseline Mean Outcome 741.5'. The outcome is a throughput measure (more pushes = more productivity), so larger_is_better. Sample sizes: N0=27 control units, N1=120 treatment units across 13 time periods (11 pre-treatment, 2 post-treatment), total observations=2352. SC and SDID methods also reported but DID is most straightforward for extraction.
source_quotes:
Panel A. Num Pushes per 100k
Chat GPT Available 899.268***
(147.395)
This design consists of 120 treatment units and 27 control units; 11 pre-treatment periods and 2 treatment periods.
The DID estimation shows an increase of approximately 899.268 pushes, which is highly significant with a standard error of 147.395.
Panel A outcome is The number of times developers in a given economy uploaded code to GitHub per 100,000 people.

computed.notes: Primary outcome is 'Num Pushes per 100k' (git pushes per 100,000 people). Table 6.1 Panel A reports DID coefficient of 899.268*** (SE=147.395) with baseline mean of 741.5. This is a level-transformed outcome (not log), so the coefficient represents the absolute increase in pushes per 100k. The baseline mean is explicitly stated as 'Baseline Mean Outcome 741.5'. The outcome is a throughput measure (more pushes = more productivity), so larger_is_better. Sample sizes: N0=27 control units, N1=120 treatment units across 13 time periods (11 pre-treatment, 2 post-treatment), total observations=2352.
